commit.message:
release: Claude Code Spot VM + branching docs (#182)

* feat: add Terraform-managed Claude Code Spot VM for 24/7 async development

Provision a GCP Spot VM that runs Claude Code CLI as a systemd daemon,
polling GitHub issues labelled 'claude' and autonomously implementing
changes on feature branches with strict SDLC compliance.

Infrastructure (all gated behind var.claude_dev_enabled):
- Spot VM (e2-standard-4) with SSD, auto-STOP on preemption
- Secret Manager secrets for Anthropic API key and GitHub PAT
- Cloud Function + Cloud Scheduler for auto-restart every 30 min
- IAM binding for Secret Manager access

SDLC enforcement:
- Agent only pushes to feature/* branches, never develop or main
- Tests must pass before PR creation; failures abort with a comment
- GitHub branch protection rules block direct pushes (documented)
- Conventional Commits enforced via prompt injection

Files:
- terraform/claude_dev_vm.tf — all Terraform resources
- terraform/scripts/claude-vm-startup.sh — VM bootstrap script
- terraform/functions/claude_dev_restarter/ — restart Cloud Function
- scripts/claude-agent-loop.js — agent daemon (polls issues, runs Claude)
- docs/CLAUDE_CODE_SPOT_VM.md — full operational plan

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add Claude Code Spot VM for 24/7 autonomous development (#180)

* feat: use Claude Pro subscription auth instead of API key for spot VM

Remove anthropic-api-key secret from Terraform and startup script.
Claude Code now authenticates via Pro subscription OAuth session
(persists on boot disk across preemptions). One-time setup: SSH in
and run `sudo -u claude-bot claude login`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: switch betfair_vm to metadata map to prevent forced replacement

Using metadata = { startup-script = ... } instead of
metadata_startup_script avoids destroying and recreating the
production VM when Terraform reconciles imported state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: install Claude Code natively to fix auto-update permissions on VM

- Use `claude install` (native) instead of `npm install -g` to avoid
  the "Insufficient permissions for auto-updates" warning caused by
  root-owned npm global installs
- Bootstrap via npm then immediately switch to native install and remove
  the npm version to avoid "Multiple installations found" warning
- Pre-create .mcp.json as bot user to fix EACCES permission denied error
- Add ~/.local/bin to PATH in bot user profile and systemd service

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix input/app label using lookup map instead of selectedOptions

sdpi-select is a custom element that doesn't expose selectedOptions,
so reading the label from it always fell back to the raw ID (e.g.
HDMI_1). Now a label map is built when the list is populated and
looked up on change, so the human-readable label is saved and shown
on the key.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: allow internal key auth on inbox monitor endpoints

The inbox monitor start/stop/status endpoints required JWT auth, but
ADK tools call them with X-Internal-Key header. Added dual auth
dependency that accepts either JWT Bearer or X-Internal-Key with
explicit tenant_id param, following the existing remedia.py pattern.

commit.message:
fix(tmux): enable buffer-based delivery for Claude Code multi-line prompts

send-keys -l silently strips newlines (tmux 3.6+), while paste-buffer
converts them to CR which terminal apps interpret as Enter. Claude Code
was excluded from buffer-based sending, causing multi-line auto-injected
prompts to arrive as a single concatenated string without submission.

Add AgentClaude case to needsBufferSend() matching the existing
AgentGemini pattern: use buffer when content contains newlines.

Closes #86

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(installer): use .DirectoryName for provider-neutral temp path on Windows

New-TemporaryFile | Split-Path can produce provider-qualified paths like
Microsoft.PowerShell.Core\FileSystem::C:\... on some PowerShell hosts.
ZipFile::ExtractToDirectory rejects these paths with NotSupportedException.

Using (New-TemporaryFile).DirectoryName returns a clean filesystem path.

Closes #104

commit.message:
Fix dev card panel visibility and trade UX

Dev cards: move panel to top of action panel (above dice/build) so it
is always the first thing visible without scrolling. Added description
text for each card type so the player always knows what their card does.

Trade: switch from batch-based counting to individual-unit counting.
Each tap on a Give resource adds exactly 1 unit (not a full ratio batch),
so players with e.g. 2 ore can see their count building even before
reaching the 4:1 threshold. A credit indicator shows how many credits
the current offer earns, or explains how many more resources are needed
for the next credit. The Get section only appears once at least 1 credit
is earned. On execute, only the ratio-valid portion is deducted (no waste).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix build errors: chart types, resizable imports, SSR compatibility

commit.message:
fix: geocode before bounds filter — Trestle returns null lat/lng

Trestle IDX Plus returns Latitude/Longitude as null AND blocks OData
geo filters (400 error). Fix: run Census geocoder on the full result
set BEFORE applying bounds post-filter, so coordinates are available
for the bounding box check. Previously geocoding ran after pagination
(step 4c), so bounds filter saw all nulls and kept everything.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Handle missing dist gracefully in local dev

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix vLLM+torchrun deadlock and prepare for cluster deployment

- Clear ALL torchrun env vars (by prefix) before vLLM init, not just a partial list
- Remove dead VLLM_USE_V1=0 (V0 doesn't exist in this fork)
- Set CUDA device before DDP init so NCCL doesn't collide with vLLM on cuda:0
- Fix teacher device context for Triton/Liger kernel compatibility
- Unwrap dataset batching in prepare_prompts (.iter wraps in extra list)
- Reduce MAX_CONTEXT_LENGTH to 2048 (sufficient for distillation)
- Parameterize nproc in launch_ddp.sh (first arg, default 2)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Implement AB$ Regenerate support (246 cards, CR 701.15a)

Full-stack implementation of the Regenerate ability type, enabling 246
cards including Drudge Skeletons, Sedge Troll, and others.

## What's Implemented

- **Parsing**: ApiType::Regenerate in ability_parser.rs
- **Effect**: Effect::Regenerate variant with placeholder targeting
- **Runtime mechanics** (CR 701.15a):
  - Regeneration shields on Card (regeneration_shields: u8)
  - DestroyPermanent checks for regen shield before destruction
  - Combat damage lethal check intercepts with regen shield
  - Shield consumption: tap, clear damage, remove from combat
- **AI evaluation**: +20 creature value for Regenerate ability
- **AI timing**: Activates regeneration during combat phases
- **End-of-turn cleanup**: Damage removal + shield reset in cleanup_step
  (also implemented the previously-TODO damage removal for all creatures)

## Gameplay Evidence

```
$ cargo run --release --bin mtg -- tui decks/regeneration_test.dck \
    decks/grizzly_bears.dck --p1-draw="Swamp;Drudge Skeletons;Swamp;Swamp" \
    --seed 42 --p1=heuristic --p2=heuristic

  AI-Heuristic1 casts Drudge Skeletons (17) (putting on stack)
  Drudge Skeletons (17) resolves
  Drudge Skeletons (17) enters the battlefield as a 1/1 creature
  AI-Heuristic1 declares Drudge Skeletons (17) (1/1) as attacker
  Drudge Skeletons activates ability: Regenerate CARDNAME.
  Drudge Skeletons (17) gains a regeneration shield
  Drudge Skeletons (17) deals 1 damage to AI-Heuristic2 (life: 19)
```

The AI correctly casts Drudge Skeletons, attacks with it, and proactively
activates the regeneration ability during combat for protection.

## Test Results Summary

880 tests passed (0 failed, 0 skipped):
- 4 new regeneration mechanical tests (keywords.rs):
  shield prevents destroy, shield consumed only once, effect adds shield,
  removes from combat
- 3 new creature evaluation tests (creature_evaluation_test.rs):
  Drudge Skeletons scoring, Sedge Troll scoring, regeneration value boost

## Relationship to Java Forge

- Java Forge: Regeneration is handled in GameAction.java with
  regeneration shields tracked on GameEntity. The "Regenerate" API type
  is defined in ApiType.java and converted via AbilityFactory.
- Rust: Similar architecture with Effect::Regenerate, regeneration_shields
  on Card, and shield interception in both DestroyPermanent and combat
  damage resolution. The Rust version also clears shields in cleanup_step
  matching Java's EndOfTurn handling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: critical security and quality improvements from V1 anti-pattern audit

Security fix (CRITICAL):
- TotpService: Add tenant isolation check to backup code deletion.
  Previously used IgnoreQueryFilters() without TenantId check, allowing
  potential cross-tenant backup code deletion.

Quality fix (V1 anti-pattern elimination):
- PasskeyService: Replace bare bool returns with (bool, string?) tuples.
  DeletePasskeyAsync and RenamePasskeyAsync now return error context
  instead of silent false (V1's most common anti-pattern).
- PasskeysController: Updated to handle new tuple return types.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(server): extract excerpt from first ## section, not content after H1 [BUG-001]

The excerpt was drawn from content between the H1 title and the first ##
heading (typically a > **Value:** blockquote), instead of from within the
first ## section's content. Now extractMeta() looks for the first ## heading
and excerpts from its content, falling back to the old behavior when no ##
section exists.

Fix serpentine snake path — extend to wall before U-turn

Each row's connector now extends the timeline line all the way to the
container edge before making the 90° turn, matching the visual described:
- LTR→RTL: line goes to right wall, smooth U-turn, comes back left into RTL row
- LTR→LTR (day change): right wall then full traverse to left wall
- RTL→LTR (day change): line goes to left wall, smooth U-turn, back right
- RTL→RTL (3rd row): left wall then full traverse to right wall

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add TLS benchmark report, e2e latency canary, and dashboard widgets

benchmark-2026-03-07b.md: detailed TLS 1.2 vs TLS 1.3 cold-connection
comparison (20 samples each), keep-alive throughput up to 15,628 TPS,
no-keepalive throughput at ~1,000 TPS, and canary e2e measurements.
Key finding: TLS 1.2 benefits from OpenSSL False Start (6ms cold conn)
vs TLS 1.3 (51ms cold conn); both identical at ~0.9ms with keep-alive.

deploy/e2e-latency-canary.yaml: CronJob running every minute from the
general node (i-0f59535d0c9dc6cfc). Measures 10 HTTPS round trips per
run, publishes e2e_latency_ms and e2e_latency_p99_ms to CloudWatch
NitroEncSvc/Dev with dimension type=keepalive_canary. Fixed IMDS hop
limit on general node (1→2) to allow pod credential access.

terraform/iam.tf: add general_node_cloudwatch_metrics inline policy
granting cloudwatch:PutMetricData scoped to NitroEncSvc/Dev namespace.

CloudWatch dashboard NitroEncSvc-Dev: two new widgets at y=18 —
"End-to-End Latency Canary" (avg + p99 timeseries) and "Latency
Comparison" (in-enclave 0.015ms vs e2e canary side by side).

commit.message:
Skip bundle-path remotes during import, clean stale git remotes

Import now detects bit's internal bundle remotes (.git/bundles/<name>.bundle)
and removes them from git instead of registering as metadata-only. These are
cloud transport artifacts whose actual remote URL is lost on export.

repository.name: bit
repository.description: Version control for large files. Git tracks metadata, rclone syncs content. No server required — works with any storage backend (Google Drive, USB drives, NAS). Diff-based sync, device-aware remotes, conflict resolution. Written in Haskell.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix drawing not appearing on all canvases in Doodldool

Root cause: stroke coordinates were sent as absolute CSS pixels from the
drawer's canvas. If the receiver had a different canvas size (different
screen/window), strokes appeared in wrong positions or off-screen. Flood
fills targeted wrong pixels. Brush sizes were visually inconsistent.

Fix: normalize all coordinates to 0-1 range before sending over the
network. The Canvas now sends normalized coords via onStroke and its
imperative drawSegment/floodFill methods accept normalized coords,
converting to local canvas size on receive. Local drawing continues
using raw CSS coords directly.

commit.message:
Fix ExecStart to use 'nanobot gateway' subcommand, add sudo to read commands

The systemd service was running bare `nanobot` with no subcommand, causing
it to print help and exit in a crash loop. Also all nanomanager commands
that read state.yaml need sudo since it's mode 600.
